title: cotula pedunculata
intro: Cotula pedunculata, also known as common buttonweed, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and antifungal effects, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Cotula pedunculata include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Cotula pedunculata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
